failedAssertions.reason: VM guest kernel panics during boot: 'VFS: Unable to mount root fs on unknown-block(0,0)'. Root cause: kernel config missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y. Firecracker specifies virtio-mmio device via kernel cmdline (virtio_mmio.device=4K@0xc0001000:6) but kernel ignores it without this option, so /dev/vda is never created.
End-to-end flow blocked: VM guest fails to boot (kernel panic). Gateway is healthy with Z.AI provider and ready to route, but no healthy VM guest to complete the path.
Cannot test guest isolation: VM guest fails to boot due to kernel panic (missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y). Guest never reaches running state.
Cannot verify guest secret absence: VM guest fails to boot (kernel panic). Guest image design excludes provider credentials but cannot be verified without running VM.

rootCauseAnalysis.issue: Firecracker VM guest kernel panics during boot - cannot mount root filesystem
rootCauseAnalysis.kernelPanicMessage: VFS: Unable to mount root fs on unknown-block(0,0)
symptoms: VM ownership created successfully in registry
Firecracker process starts but exits immediately after kernel panic
Health check connection refused on guest ports (9000, 9001, etc.)
Kernel has VIRTIO_MMIO=y, VIRTIO_BLK=y, but missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y
Kernel sees virtio_mmio.device= parameters but doesn't create /dev/vda

kernelConfigGap.whyItMatters: Firecracker uses kernel command line to specify virtio-mmio device addresses (virtio_mmio.device=4K@0xc0001000:6). Without VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES, the kernel ignores these parameters and doesn't instantiate the block device.

rootCauseAnalysis.kernelBootArgs: console=ttyS0 reboot=k panic=1 pci=off root=/dev/vda rw init=/bin/init guest_port=8085 persistent=/mnt/persistent vm_id=<vm-id> epoch=1 ip=172.1.0.2::172.1.0.1:255.255.255.252::eth0:off pci=off root=/dev/vda rw virtio_mmio.device=4K@0xc0001000:6 virtio_mmio.device=4K@0xc0002000:7
rootCauseAnalysis.fixRequired: Add VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES = yes to nix/guest-image.nix kernel config, rebuild guest image (vmlinux), redeploy to Node B /var/lib/go-choir/guest/

improvements: Kernel config fixed for init script, IP_PNP, networking utilities
iptables rules properly configured in vmmanager
Gateway token flow working (vmctl issues tokens to VMs)
Firecracker process starts successfully (but kernel panics immediately)
progressSinceLastRound.remainingBlocker: Kernel config missing VIRTIO_MMIO_CMDLINE_DEVICES=y prevents virtio-mmio block device from being created, causing kernel panic on root mount

notes: Node B redeployed to commit 214b499 with 'Fix Firecracker guest health and networking'
All services running: auth, proxy, gateway (Z.AI provider with 13 identities), vmctl, sandbox
VM ownership registry works correctly - creates ownership records with proper sandbox URLs
Firecracker process starts but kernel panics immediately due to missing /dev/vda
Gateway is fully operational and ready to route - only blocked by VM guest health
Fix is straightforward: add single kernel config option and rebuild guest image
Once kernel boots successfully, all 4 assertions should be testable and likely pass
